decisions.title: Commit to Parquet as primary storage format for Explore V2
decisions.context: ENG-9255 designing Parquet schema. Critical for Explore V2 performance/scale. Impacts data model decisions across ingestion, storage, query layers. Need alignment before implementation.

arguments.for: Industry standard, excellent compression, columnar queries, proven at scale. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar for Explore performance.
arguments.against: Migration complexity from existing formats, potential breaking changes, team learning curve, schema evolution constraints.

decisions.title: Prioritize E2 stability issues vs new Canvas features
decisions.context: 9+ E2 bugs (calc channels, table values, axis controls, dynamic windows, annotations). Canvas is key 2026 product but E2 instability hurts 'Decisions Captured' metric. Resource tradeoff.

arguments.for: E2 bugs block existing customers from making decisions. Fix quality before adding features. 'Nail the Core' means current products work reliably.
arguments.against: Canvas is differentiated moat-builder. Competitors advancing. E2 bugs affect minority of workflows. Innovation over maintenance.

decisions.title: Approve GetStructuredData API performance optimization scope
decisions.context: ENG-8958 reports latency issues. Ask SIFT and Canvas query performance critical for user experience. Need to decide: quick wins vs architectural redesign.
decisions.blockedBy: Performance benchmarking data not yet collected. Need baseline metrics and user impact analysis.

arguments.for: Query speed directly impacts 'Embed Deeper' - faster insights = more decisions. Foundation for Python Rules execution.
arguments.against: Premature optimization without data. May need full data layer redesign which conflicts with Parquet migration timing.

decisions.title: Define policy management GA readiness criteria
decisions.context: ENG-5959 building Policy/Entity Attribute Management GUI. Core governance feature for 'Sift Edge' enterprise offering. Need definition of done for GA launch.

arguments.for: Clear criteria prevent scope creep. Enterprise customers need robust RBAC for compliance. Unlocks high-value accounts.
arguments.against: Premature to define GA when foundation incomplete. Market requirements still emerging. Better to ship MVP and iterate.

timeAudit.weekSummary: High engineering coordination load evident: 30 Linear issues active spanning infrastructure (catalog, ingestion), core product (E2 bugs), and strategic initiatives (Parquet, governance). 22 meetings suggest context-switching overhead.
alerts: E2 bug cluster (9 issues) indicates quality debt accumulation - consider bug bash sprint
Infrastructure issues (catalog disk errors, WAL retention OOMs) risk production stability
Only 8 free hours this week for strategic thinking - decision quality may suffer
